markdown:
## Ranking from easiest → hardest, by reported model success percentages

**1. GAIA — easiest** (highest reported model rates)
The strongest LLM configuration, GPT-4 with plugins, reaches **30.3% on Level 1, 9.7% on Level 2, and 0% on Level 3**. Other LLM runs on the same benchmark average much lower across levels: GPT-4 alone averages 9.1% on Level 1 / 2.6% on Level 2 / 0% on Level 3; GPT-4 Turbo hits 13.0% on Level 1 / 5.5% on Level 2 / 0% on Level 3; AutoGPT (GPT-4 backend) gets 14.4% on Level 1 / 0.4% on Level 2 / 0% on Level 3. Human annotators score 93.9% / 91.8% / 87.3%. [[evidence_id]]
[1]

**2. WebArena** (intermediate model rates)
GPT-4 with chain-of-thought prompting attains an end-to-end task success rate of **11.70%**, the weakest of WebArena's LLM runs (TEXT-BISON-001) gets **5.05%**, and the best LLM configuration reported in the paper (GPT-4 with the unachievable-task hint removed) reaches **14.41%**. Human performance is 78.24%. [[evidence_id]]
[2]

**3. SWE-bench — hardest** (lowest reported model rates)
On the main BM25-retrieval setting, the best-performing model, **Claude 2, resolves only 1.96% of issues**. Other main-table entries are even lower (ChatGPT-3.5 0.17%; SWE-Llama 7b/13b 0.70% each). Even with the strongest evaluation configuration reported in the paper, "oracle"-collapsed retrieval, Claude 3 Opus reaches only **9.39%**, Claude 2 **5.93%**, and GPT-4 **3.40%**. [[evidence_id]]
[3]

### Summary table (best LLM success rate reported in each paper)

| Rank | Benchmark | Best LLM success rate reported |
|------|-----------|---------------------------------|
| Easiest (1) | GAIA | 30.3% (GPT-4 + plugins, Level 1) |
| Middle (2) | WebArena | 14.41% (GPT-4, no-UA-hint setting) |
| Hardest (3) | SWE-bench | 1.96% (Claude 2, main BM25); 3.79% (Claude 3 Opus, BM25); 9.39% only under non-realistic oracle-collapsed retrieval |

The ordering is robust: SWE-bench's strongest model sits well below WebArena's weakest LLM (5.05%), and WebArena's peak (14.41%) is below GAIA's Level-1 ceiling (30.3%). Caveat: the three benchmarks measure different task families (general-assistant Q&A, web navigation, and GitHub-issue patching), so the percentages aren't a strictly apples-to-apples difficulty scale — but taken strictly as reported model success rates, the ordering GAIA > WebArena > SWE-bench holds.

Sources
[1] GAIA:, D Extended description of our question design framework, p. 21
[2] WE BAR E N A: A REALISTIC WEB ENVIRONMENT FOR BUILDING AUTONOMOUS AGENTS, 5 RESULTS, p. 8
[3] SWE-BENCH: CAN LANGUAGE MODELS RESOLVE REAL-WORLD GITHUB ISSUES?, 5 RESULTS, p. 6
